meaning: n.保护者,(博物馆的)管理员,(公共福利的)监督官
examples: If an arc forms, gas accumulation is rapid and oil flows rapidly into the conservator.
If an arc forms, gas accumulation is rapid, and oil flows rapidly into the conservator.
Take a look at the table and try to open the box, but Sylvie will want to ask the conservator for permission first.
Hilton can challenge that authority by seeking the appointment of a guardian or conservator in court.
The value dropped dramatically after a car conservator society raised questions about its authenticity.
